creator: notstop@live.com
text:
On 19/07/2012 19:05, Johan van Selst wrote:
> notstop wrote:
>> I don't know what specific system or compilation you use, but I've
>> experienced the issue with PCBSD9.1-BETA1-x86-DVD.iso live. Just
>> `pkg_add -r mcrypt`.
>
> I ran it on several systems, including 9-STABLE/amd64 and 8-STABLE/i386;
> it works just fine on my computers. There might be a problem with the
> combination of libraries and binary versions on your system.
>
> If you have a ports tree available, could you try to compile mcrypt
> locally and then run it again? Also, please tell me more about your
> system, including the output of 'uname' and 'pkg_version -vs mcrypt'
> and the exact error message that this command gives you.
>
> To install mcrypt from ports, you can use the commands:
> # pkg_delete mcrypt\* libmcrypt\*
> # make -C /usr/ports/security/mcrypt/ install clean
>
>
> Best regards,
> Johan
>

There are the attachments of error with bot pkg_add (binary precompiled 
I presume) and both portsnap version. Both behave similar, don't decode 
well 'echo a' or 'echo aaa' or similar. This has been testes with the 
PCBSD9.1-BETA1-x86-DVD.iso just installed on the system.
